channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: Big US Solar and Battery Project Lines Up $3.5 Billion Financing
item.description: Renewables developer Cypress Creek Energy closed on a $3.5 billion financing for one of the biggest solar and battery projects in the US, a sign of sustained investor interest amid federal pushback against clean energy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Chediak

item.title: Hanuman Wind Pulls Bond Again as Parent Governance Clouds
item.description: Hanuman Wind Power, a Hong Kong-based unit of troubled Thai renewable energy firm Energy Absolute Pcl, postponed a junk bond offering for a second time after it failed to attract the desired investor demand, according to a person familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified.
item.dc:creator: Anuchit Nguyen, Dipika Lalwani

item.title: UK Venture Plans Europe’s Largest Plant for Capturing CO2 From Air
item.description: UnionDAC, the name of the new company, aims to become a leader in removing carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere
item.dc:creator: Alastair Marsh

item.title: New Zealand Sees $2.9 Billion Cost to Use Carbon Credits for Climate Goal
item.description: New Zealand could need to pay as much as NZ$5 billion ($2.9 billion) if it purchases overseas carbon credits to help meet 2030 emissions-cutting targets under the Paris Agreement.
item.dc:creator: Ainsley Thomson

item.title: Ben Cohen Regrets Making Ben & Jerry’s Public
item.dc:creator: Akshat Rathi

item.title: Lee Raymond, Exxon CEO Who Doubted Climate Change, Dies at 87
item.description: Lee Raymond, the former head of Exxon Mobil Corp. who oversaw the biggest corporate merger in the history of the oil industry and was derided as “the Darth Vader of global warming” for his skepticism toward climate change, has died. He was 87.
item.dc:creator: Joe Carroll, Kevin Crowley

item.title: Texas Governor Seeks Consumer Protection From Data Center Costs
item.description: Texas Governor Greg Abbott called on state regulators to better protect ratepayers from shouldering the costs of the data center buildout, the latest sign of growing political scrutiny over the AI-driven surge in electricity demand.
item.dc:creator: John Ainger

item.title: El Niño Emerges in Pacific, Raising Heat and Crop Risks
item.description: El Niño has formed across the equatorial Pacific, setting the stage for months of droughts, floods and temperature fluctuations that will threaten communities worldwide along with agriculture and energy.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Why Sustainable Finance Hasn’t Moved the Needle on Climate Change
item.description: A new study by Lisa Sachs, director of the Columbia Center on Sustainable Investment, lays out the problem.
item.dc:creator: Alastair Marsh, Olivia Raimonde, Frances Schwartzkopff

item.title: NYC, Northeast Face Sweltering Heat as World Cup Matches Begin
item.description: Hot weather will settle over New York and the US Northeast through the end of the week, bringing temperatures in the 90s to several cities hosting FIFA World Cup matches this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan
